title: New Training available: Military Mobility and the use of Form 302
content: Discover the new available training for Military Mobility and the use of Form 302
snippet: Discover the new available training for Military Mobility and the use of Form 302

title: EBRD and EU step up support for digital and green investments
content: The European Bank for Reconstruction and Development (EBRD) and the European Union (EU) are stepping up their support for the green transition across the Western Balkans, and digital development across the regions outside the EU where the EBRD operates.
snippet: The European Bank for Reconstruction and Development (EBRD) and the European Union (EU) are stepping up their support for the green transition across the Western Balkans, and digital development

title: Commission approves €100 million Austrian State aid scheme to support cleantech manufacturing capacity
content: The European Commission has approved a €100 million Austrian scheme to support clean technology ('cleantech') manufacturing capacity, in line with the objectives of the Clean Industrial Deal. This measure will contribute to the transition towards a net-zero economy.
snippet: The European Commission has approved a €100 million Austrian scheme to support clean technology ('cleantech') manufacturing capacity, in line with the objectives of the Clean Industrial Deal. This

title: Commission endorses Lithuania’s €884 million Social Climate Plan to support vulnerable households and micro-enterprises in the clean transition
content: The European Commission has adopted Lithuania’s Social Climate Plan, the second one under the new Social Climate Fund.
snippet: The European Commission has adopted Lithuania’s Social Climate Plan, the second one under the new Social Climate Fund.

title: EU Steel Regulation: Commission launches consultation on the type of evidence to be provided on country of ‘melt and pour’
content: Today, the European Commission launched a targeted consultation seeking stakeholders’ feedback on the appropriate documentation for implementing the country ‘melt and pour’ traceability requirements under the EU Steel Regulation.
snippet: Today, the European Commission launched a targeted consultation seeking stakeholders’ feedback on the appropriate documentation for implementing the country ‘melt and pour’ traceability requirements

title: Deadline extended for 2026 Excellence in Road Safety Awards
content: Recognising outstanding and innovative actions that contribute to safer roads across Europe.
snippet: Recognising outstanding and innovative actions that contribute to safer roads across Europe.

title: Amazonian Indigenous women win 2026 #ForOurPlanet prize
content: The award highlights the initiative's commitment to strengthening the leadership of Indigenous women in protecting the Amazon rainforest.
snippet: The award highlights the initiative's commitment to strengthening the leadership of Indigenous women in protecting the Amazon rainforest.

title: European Energy Exchange appointed as EU ETS common auction platform from 2027
content: On 2 June, the European Commission signed a five-year contract with European Energy Exchange AG (EEX) and its clearing system European Commodity Clearing AG (ECC) for the services of the fourth common auction platform under the EU Emissions Trading System (EU ETS).

title: European Semester Spring Package 2026: a clear call to step up research and innovation efforts across the EU
content: The Spring Package urges Member States to boost research and innovation to unlock Europe’s full potential for competitiveness and resilience.
snippet: The Spring Package urges Member States to boost research and innovation to unlock Europe’s full potential for competitiveness and resilience.

title: June infringements package: key decisions on energy
content: In its regular package of infringement decisions, the European Commission pursues legal action against Member States for failing to comply with their obligations under EU law.
snippet: In its regular package of infringement decisions, the European Commission pursues legal action against Member States for failing to comply with their obligations under EU law.

title: Shaping the workforce of tomorrow
content: The European Skills & VET Week 2026 has officially begun. The year-long campaign will serve as an umbrella for debates, activities and publications organised by stakeholders across Europe, bridging perspectives from different actors involved in the path from education to the labour market.

title: European Semester 2026: Housing in the spotlight
content: Focus on affordability, supply, and social housing as the Commission for the first time includes housing as a separate annex of the European Semester Spring Package.
snippet: Focus on affordability, supply, and social housing as the Commission for the first time includes housing as a separate annex of the European Semester Spring Package.

title: Commissioner Kos visits Moldova to advance EU accession reforms and investments
content: On Thursday and Friday, Commissioner for Enlargement, Marta Kos, is in Moldova to support with the country on its path to joining the EU. She will notably discuss the progress Moldova has made on reforms and encourage further investments ahead of the second EU-Moldova Summit, which is due to take...

title: EUAA publishes latest asylum trends in EU+ countries
content: The European Union Agency for Asylum has published its latest analysis of asylum applications. It shows that in March 2026, EU+ countries received around 59,000 applications for international protection, broadly stable compared to February and below the levels recorded throughout most of 2025.

title: Executive Vice-President Ribera and Commissioner Jørgensen in Finland to discuss clean energy transition and housing affordability
content: This week, both Executive Vice-President for Clean, Just and Competitive Transition Teresa Ribera and Commissioner for Energy and Housing Dan Jørgensen are visiting Finland to discuss electrification, energy security, affordability and competitiveness.
